channel.title: flove making-of — backend questions
channel.description: New backend design questions from flove interviews.

item.title: How should routing.json be authored?
item.description:
• Community wiki — Anyone can edit. Democratic, fast updates, risk of vandalism. • Curator-only — Only curators can edit. Safe, slow, controlled. • Automated (AI) — AI generates/maintains routing. Fast, unpredictable, hard to audit. • Marc-only — Only Marc edits. Simplest, most control.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Marc-only

item.title: Should cross-links be typed or untyped?
item.description:
• Typed — Defined relationship types (responds_to, derived_from). Richer querying, more schema. • Untyped — Simple hyperlinks. Simpler, less metadata, still useful. • Hybrid — Untyped by default, optional type annotation. Flexible.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Untyped

item.title: Should flove have per-app cube downloads?
item.description:
• Yes, per-app cubes — Each app gets its own zip. Users download what they need. Needs builder script. • No, flove.zip only — One big bundle. Simpler, but larger download. • Defer — Don't build yet — revisit when more apps exist.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Defer

item.title: What AI fine-tuning approach for module 42?
item.description:
• Full fine-tuning — Train entire model. Best quality, highest cost. • LoRA — Low-rank adaptation. Cheaper, nearly as good, less control. • No fine-tuning — Use base model as-is. Free, least customized. • Defer — Don't decide yet — wait until module 42 is closer to shipping.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Defer

item.title: What granularity for visibility controls?
item.description:
• Per-element — Each element has its own visibility. Maximum control, maximum complexity. • 0asis defaults — Global defaults, no per-element override. Simple, less flexible. • Hybrid — Default from 0asis, with per-element override option. Balanced.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Hybrid
